title: 10 best herbal tinctures for cough

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with cough because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled or taken as a supplement, this compound can help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and relieving coughing spasms. Additionally, licorice tinctures may also help thin mucus, making it easier to expel and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which helps to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing the severity of coughing.

Flavonoids and polyphenols: These bioactive compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, making it easier to manage coughing fits.
Saponins: These natural compounds have expectorant properties, which helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in treating coughs.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids, which have antitussive properties, helping to relieve coughs and reduce inflammation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 70% vodka in a glass jar at a ratio of 1:4.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, allowing the mixture to steep and infuse.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any solids and discard the roots.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine tinctures helps with cough because its active compounds, such as limonene and beta-pinene, possess natural expectorant properties. When ingested, these compounds help to loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, yellow jessamine's anti-inflammatory properties may reduce throat irritation and discomfort associated with coughing. By soothing and calming the respiratory system, yellow jessamine tinctures offer a natural remedy for alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antitussive and Expectorant: Yellow Jessamine tincture helps to relieve coughing by suppressing the urge to cough while also loosening and expelling mucus from the lungs, making it easier to clear out.

Anti-Inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of yellow Jessamine tincture help reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate congestion and coughing caused by infection or allergies.

Antiviral and Antibacterial: Yellow Jessamine tincture contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain viruses and bacteria, which can help combat infections that cause coughs and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Yellow Jessamine contain alkaloids and flavonoids that help to relieve cough symptoms by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways.
Barks: The bark of Yellow Jessamine is rich in glycosides, which are responsible for its antitussive properties, helping to soothe and calm the cough reflex.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gelsemium sempervirens leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop 2 ounces of harvested plant material into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with 100 proof vodka leaving about an inch at the top for expansion during infusion.
Store the jar in a dark cupboard for four to six weeks shaking it daily to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with cough because they contain bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids that possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the airways, loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs and bronchi. As a result, elder tinctures can provide rapid relief from coughing fits, congestion, and chest tightness, making them a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and colds.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elder tincture contains antiviral compounds that help combat the underlying viral infection causing the cough, reducing its severity and duration.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate coughing and make it more difficult to breathe.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in elder tincture help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have a rich concentration of flavonoids and terpenes that help to soothe and calm irritated throats, making them an effective ingredient in cough tinctures.

Barks: The bark of the elder plant contains anthocyanins and other polyphenols that provide anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, making it a popular choice for cough remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra leaves flowers and berries in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly chop or crush the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine one part chopped plant material with 4 parts vodka or rum in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily for infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and transfer to a dark-topped bottle for long-term storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with cough because it contains compounds like ursolic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways. The tincture also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and congestion. Additionally, purple coneflower has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to expel, providing relief from stubborn coughs.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Purple coneflower tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses responsible for respiratory infections, including those that cause coughs, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can alleviate coughing fits and make breathing easier.

Mucolytic properties: Purple coneflower contains mucolytic agents that can break down excess mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing associated with respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including saponins and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antitussive and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for relieving cough symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the purple coneflower contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning when dew is present for best potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 135°f for 6 hours or air dry them for 7-10 days.
Combine 1 part dried echinacea flowers with 4 parts 95% ethyl alcohol in a clean glass jar and let it infuse for 4 weeks.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store the finished tincture at room temperature for up to 2 years, taking 20-30 drops three times daily as needed for cough relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles surrounding the bronchial tubes, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme's antibacterial properties help to combat infections that can cause coughs, providing fast relief from congestion and discomfort. As a result, herbal thyme tinctures are a popular natural remedy for alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antitussive property: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, helps to suppress the urge to cough, providing relief from dry and irritating coughs.
Antimicrobial property: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections caused by bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can lead to coughs, reducing their severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to coughing and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of essential oils, which are effective in reducing cough and relieving congestion.
Leaves: Fresh or dried thyme leaves possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for soothing irritated respiratory tracts and alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning when they are at their peak potency for respiratory health.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Combine 100g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the bottle with the date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The menthol in peppermint oil also relaxes the muscles in the throat and chest, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving congestion. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling in the airways, further alleviating coughing fits and making breathing easier. Overall, herbal peppermint tinctures provide a natural and effective way to soothe and calm a nagging cough.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's primary effect is to stimulate the mucous membranes, helping to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion and coughing.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has natural decongestant properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieving coughs and congestion caused by colds, bronchitis, and asthma.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have a calming and anti-inflammatory effect, making them effective in relieving cough symptoms.
Leaves: Fresh peppermint leaves contain menthol and other compounds that help soothe an irritated throat and alleviate coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality mentha x piperita leaves by cutting fresh sprigs in the morning for maximum essential oil content.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a dehydrator or air circulation method at 90°f for 1-2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried peppermint leaves into small pieces and combine with 100% organic vodka at a ratio of 1:2 (one part herb to two parts liquid).
Store the mixture in a dark glass jar and allow it to steep at room temperature for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily, to extract compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with cough because its natural compounds, particularly anethole, have a soothing effect on the respiratory system. When taken internally, fennel tincture helps to relax the muscles in the throat and bronchi, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with coughs. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, thus providing fast relief from a persistent cough.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and making it easier to cough up phlegm.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the airways, which can help to alleviate coughing caused by inflammation or irritation in the throat.
Antispasmodic property: Fennel's antispasmodic compounds help to relax the muscles in the airways and throat, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a dry, irritated cough.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain mucilages that can help thin out mucus and ease congestion caused by coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves or purchase dried fennel herb, measuring 2 cups for this recipe.
Chop the fresh fennel leaves or crush the dried fennel herb into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the chopped fennel with 80-proof vodka, covering the mixture by at least an inch, in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it daily, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the vodka.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids, and transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese snakegourd tinctures helps with cough because its unique blend of natural compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, works to soothe and calm irritated airways. The tincture's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the herbal formula has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, further alleviating cough symptoms. As a result, Chinese snakegourd tinctures provide effective relief from coughs and other respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cucurbitacins, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating cough symptoms.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in the tincture can loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and reducing the severity of a cough.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The tincture's antiviral and antibacterial compounds help combat infections that cause coughs, such as common colds and flu, by inhibiting the growth of viruses and bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, roots of Chinese Snakegourd have been traditionally used to soothe coughs and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the plant, rhyzomes contain volatile oils and phenolic compounds that help to expel phlegm and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality trichosanthes kirilowii roots and cut them into small pieces.
Dry the root pieces in a warm and well-ventilated area for several days.
Grind the dried root pieces into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered root with 1000 milliliters of vodka or 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 14 to 21 days before straining and decanting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tinctures helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation. The tincture's expectorant qualities help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel, providing relief from congestion. Additionally, skullcap contains flavonoids, which have a bronchodilatory effect, opening up airways and improving breathing. By reducing coughing fits and relieving respiratory discomfort, herbal skullcap tinctures provide natural and effective relief for people suffering from chronic or acute coughs.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Skullcap's tannins and flavonoids help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Skullcap's bioactive compounds, such as baicalin and wogonin, exhibit anti-inflammatory activity that helps to reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and cough less.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in skullcap tinctures help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make a soothing and calming tincture that helps to relieve cough symptoms by reducing inflammation and spasms in the respiratory tract.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids, which help to ease coughs and soothe irritated throats by reducing inflammation and relaxing airway muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried scutellaria lateriflora flowers and leaves in sufficient quantity.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine one part of the powdered skullcap with two parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least 14 days to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and label it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with cough because its soothing properties can provide instant relief from annoying tickles in the throat. The tannins present in mullein help to reduce inflammation and swelling, which can be a major contributor to chronic coughing. Additionally, the expectorant properties of mullein help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier for your body to expel it and reducing the frequency and severity of coughs. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to calm coughs and soothe irritated throats, providing a safe and effective solution for a variety of respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Mullein tincture contains a high amount of mucilage, which is a thick, protective substance that coats and soothes the throat and bronchial passages, reducing inflammation and irritation, and providing relief from coughing.

Antitussive properties: The bioactive compounds present in mullein, such as rosmarinic acid, have been shown to possess antitussive properties, which helps to reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits, making it an effective natural remedy for coughs.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Mullein tincture also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, further alleviating cough symptoms such as congestion, wheezing, and chest tightness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and saponins, which aid in reducing inflammation and expelling mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mullein leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day.
Chop the mullein parts into small pieces then combine with 70 percent ethanol.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place to allow tincture formation.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard solids after one hour of standing.
Transfer the clear herbal tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for cough are liquid extracts made from plants, flowers, and herbs that have been traditionally used to soothe and calm coughs. These natural remedies help alleviate symptoms by reducing inflammation and congestion in the throat and chest, making it easier to breathe and sleep. Examples of effective herbal tinctures for cough include thyme, ginger, and eucalyptus. By using these tinctures, people can find relief from chronic coughing, improve their quality of life, and reduce reliance on over-the-counter medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cough with herbal tinctures is to combine different formulas for optimal relief. For dry or tickling coughs, try a blend of thyme, eucalyptus, and mullein tinctures taken in 20-30 drops, three times a day. For chesty or productive coughs, mix equal parts of marshmallow, licorice root, and slippery elm tinctures for soothing relief. Consult with an herbalist or healthcare professional to create a personalized formula based on individual needs and health status.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cough that are treated with herbal tinctures are bronchitis, tonsillitis, and sinusitis. Herbal tinctures like elderberry, eucalyptus, and peppermint help to soothe congestion and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Other herbal remedies, such as garlic, onion, and ginger, have natural antiseptic properties that combat infection and ease symptoms. These tinctures can be used individually or combined with other herbs to provide effective relief from these respiratory issues.
